summary: Add the canonical WTS recruiter control-plane persistence layer for submitted job requests and draft lineage.
rationale: Task 0A adds durable job request revisions, draft-to-request lineage, and future workflow-start groundwork inside the conversation-agent SQLite lifecycle so later runtime-control tasks can start from canonical state instead of transcript or frontend payloads.
deletion_targets: workflow-start state inferred from transcript messages
requirement-draft lineage inferred from frontend submit payloads
job request identity represented only by ad hoc source IDs
risks: The new WTS schema must remain owned by the conversation-agent SQLite lifecycle without introducing a second PRAGMA authority.
Job request idempotency must reject conflicting bodies while allowing safe replay of the same submitted JD.
Task 0B depends on canonical field names for workflow start intents and draft-to-job-request lineage.
